info.title: cloacina-server API
info.description: REST API for the Cloacina workflow-orchestration server. WebSocket protocols (execution events, accumulator ingest, reactor commands, substrate delivery) are specified in the WebSocket Protocol reference of the documentation site.

get.summary: GET /health — liveness check (no auth, no DB)
GET /ready — readiness check (verifies DB connection pool is healthy)

200.description: Ready — DB reachable, no crashed graphs

503.description: Not ready — `reason` field explains (database unreachable / crashed computation graphs)

get.summary:
`GET /v1/agents` — operator-facing snapshot of the execution-agent fleet
roster (admin only). CLOACI-I-0124 / WS-0b. Per-replica: reflects the agents
registered against *this* server instance.

GET /auth/keys — list all API keys (no hashes or plaintext).
Requires admin role.

post.summary: POST /auth/keys — create a new API key.
post.description:
Requires admin role. Non-admin keys cannot create keys with higher
permissions than their own (prevents privilege escalation).
Returns the plaintext key exactly once. It cannot be retrieved again.

201.description: Key created — plaintext returned exactly once

delete.summary:
DELETE /auth/keys/:key_id — revoke an API key.
Requires admin role within tenant (or god mode).

404.description: Key not found or already revoked

post.summary: `POST /v1/auth/local/login` — verify a password, mint a short-TTL key.

200.description: Logged in — minted key returned once

post.summary: POST /auth/ws-ticket — exchange a Bearer token for a single-use WebSocket ticket.
post.description:
Returns a short-lived ticket that can be used as a query parameter for
WebSocket upgrade requests, avoiding long-lived API keys in URLs.

get.summary: `GET /v1/compiler/status` — build-pipeline status (admin only).

GET /v1/health/accumulators — list registered accumulators with health,
filtered by the caller's authorization. CLOACI-T-0579 / SEC-05.

200.description: Registered accumulators with health, filtered by authorization

post.summary:
POST /v1/health/accumulators/{name}/inject — push a single typed event into
a running accumulator (CLOACI-T-0753).
post.description:
Operator-facing REST analogue of the WS accumulator-push path. The typed JSON
`event` is serialized to the boundary wire encoding server-side (same framing
as the front-door accumulator socket), so operators never craft raw
`Vec<u8>`. Authorization reuses the accumulator endpoint policy — the same
gate the WS accumulator endpoint enforces. Successful injects are
audit-logged and marked `operator_injected` since they bypass the real event
source.

get.summary:
GET /v1/health/accumulators/{name}/interface — the accumulator's declared
input interface (CLOACI-I-0128 T-0758): the single boundary slot an operator
supplies to `inject`. Empty `slots` means undeclared/untyped. Read-only
discovery; the same slot backs the validation in `inject_accumulator`.

GET /v1/health/graphs — list loaded graphs visible to the caller.
CLOACI-T-0579 / SEC-05.

200.description: Loaded graphs visible to the caller

get.summary:
GET /v1/health/graphs/{name} — single graph health, gated by caller
authorization. Cross-tenant requests 404 rather than 403 so an
adversary can't probe for tenant graph names. CLOACI-T-0579.

404.description: Graph not found (or not visible to caller)

get.summary:
GET /v1/health/reactors — list loaded reactors visible to the caller
(CLOACI-T-0742). Reactor-first: includes reactors with no graph bound, which
`list_graphs` omits. Visibility reuses the same tenant gate as graphs.

200.description: Loaded reactors visible to the caller

post.summary:
POST /v1/health/reactors/{name}/fire — manually fire a running reactor
(CLOACI-T-0751).
post.description:
Operator-facing REST surface over the existing reactor `ForceFire` /
`FireWith` mechanics (previously WebSocket-only). Two modes:

- `force_fire`: fire the graph with the reactor's current cache, untouched.
- `fire_with`: replace the reactor's cache with the supplied typed `inputs`
  then fire. Full-replace only (mirrors the engine's `replace_all`); there
  is no partial/merge mode in v1.

Typed JSON `inputs` are serialized to the boundary wire encoding
server-side so operators never deal in raw `Vec<u8>`. Each source value is
encoded exactly as the front-door accumulator path encodes it: the JSON is
rendered to UTF-8 bytes, then those bytes are bincode-wrapped
(`bincode(Vec<u8>)`) — the format the passthrough accumulator and the FFI
bridge expect.

Authorization reuses the existing per-op reactor policy
(`ReactorOp::ForceFire` / `ReactorOp::FireWith`), the same gate the WS
reactor endpoint enforces. Successful fires are audit-logged and marked
`operator_injected` since they bypass the real event source.

403.description: Operation not permitted on this reactor

get.summary:
GET /v1/health/reactors/{name}/fires — recent fires with outcome + duration
(CLOACI-T-0766). Makes the reactive layer observable: what fired, did it
complete, how long, and why it failed.

GET /v1/health/reactors/{name}/fires/timeseries — fires per minute, last 60
minutes (CLOACI-T-0766), for the fire-activity heatmap.

GET /v1/health/reactors/{name}/interface — the reactor's declared input
interface (CLOACI-I-0128 T-0758): the per-source boundary slots an operator
supplies to `fire_with`. Empty `slots` means undeclared/untyped (the reactor
accepts free-form input). Read-only discovery so a UI can render a typed fire
form; the same slots back the server-side validation in `fire_reactor`.

GET /tenants — list tenant schemas.
Requires admin role — only admins can enumerate tenants.

post.summary:
POST /tenants — create a new tenant (Postgres schema + user + migrations).
Admin-only: only is_admin keys can create tenants.

delete.summary: DELETE /tenants/:schema_name — remove a tenant via orchestrated teardown.
delete.description:
CLOACI-T-0581: replaces the old single-call `admin.remove_tenant` with
the four-step top-down order:
  1. Revoke every still-active API key for the tenant (close the auth
     surface so new requests fail).
  2. Evict the tenant's `DefaultRunner` from `TenantRunnerCache`,
     awaiting its graceful shutdown (drains in-flight executions,
     stops scheduler loop, closes per-tenant DB pool).
  3. Evict the tenant's `Database` from `TenantDatabaseCache`.
  4. Drop schema + user via `DatabaseAdmin::remove_tenant`.

Each step emits a structured audit event with duration. Per-step
failures bail out — but earlier steps stay committed, so a retry
picks up where the failure occurred. Each step is idempotent.

Closes SEC-14 (stale state after delete) and SEC-17 (unbounded
caches surviving delete).

get.summary: `GET /v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/accounts` — list a tenant's local accounts.

403.description: Tenant access or admin role denied
Tenant access or admin role denied

delete.summary:
`DELETE /v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/accounts/{account_id}` — disable an account.
Disable (not hard-delete) preserves history; already-minted keys lapse at
their TTL (deprovisioning latency bounded by the short TTL).

403.description: Tenant access or admin role denied

404.description: Account not found in this tenant

403.description: Tenant access or admin role denied

404.description: Account not found in this tenant

get.summary: GET /tenants/:tenant_id/executions — list workflow executions.
get.description:
**CLOACI-T-0594 / API-02:** accepts `?status=Failed` and
`?workflow_name=foo` and `?limit=N&offset=M`. Previously these
query params were silently discarded.

get.summary: GET /tenants/:tenant_id/executions/:id — get execution details.
GET /tenants/:tenant_id/executions/:id/events — execution event log.
GET /tenants/:tenant_id/executions/:id/tasks — per-task rows for an execution.

GET /tenants/:tenant_id/keys — list keys scoped to one tenant (CLOACI-T-0784).
Tenant-admin self-service; authZ (`TenantParam + Admin`) is enforced by the
route-table middleware, so the caller is already confined to `tenant_id`.

403.description: Tenant access or admin role denied

post.summary:
POST /tenants/:tenant_id/keys — create a key scoped to a tenant.
Admin-only: only is_admin keys can create tenant-scoped keys.

201.description: Tenant-scoped key created — plaintext returned exactly once

delete.summary:
DELETE /tenants/:tenant_id/keys/:key_id — revoke a key owned by one tenant
(CLOACI-T-0784). The middleware confines the caller to `tenant_id`; this
handler additionally verifies the target key belongs to that tenant before
revoking. A cross-tenant or unknown id is reported as not-found (never
revoked) so a tenant-admin can't probe or touch another tenant's keys.

403.description: Tenant access or admin role denied

404.description: Key not found in this tenant

get.summary: GET /tenants/:tenant_id/triggers — list all schedules (cron + trigger).
get.description:
CLOACI-T-0579: routed through the tenant-scoped `Database` from
`TenantDatabaseCache` so the underlying `SELECT FROM schedules`
hits the tenant's schema, not the admin schema. Closes SEC-02.

get.summary: GET /tenants/:tenant_id/triggers/:name — trigger details + recent executions.
get.description:
CLOACI-T-0579: routed through the tenant-scoped `Database`. A request
for tenant B with a trigger id that belongs to tenant A naturally
404s — the row simply doesn't exist in tenant B's schema. No
info-disclosure via "not in your tenant" error code. Closes SEC-02.

post.summary:
POST /tenants/:tenant_id/triggers/:name/fire — manually fire a trigger,
fanning out to every subscribed workflow (CLOACI-T-0777). One operator action
instead of running each workflow by hand. An optional `event` is merged into
each fired workflow's context (alongside trigger metadata). The started
executions are marked `manual` (CLOACI-T-0776).

404.description: No enabled subscribers for this trigger

get.summary:
GET /tenants/:tenant_id/triggers/:name/interface — the trigger's declared
pass-through schema (CLOACI-T-0777 P2): the union of the declared params of
every workflow subscribed to this trigger. Empty `slots` means an untyped
trigger (free-form event). Read-only discovery; the same slots back the
validation in `fire_trigger`, and the UI builds a typed fire form from them.

post.description:
Resolves the schedule by trigger name or workflow name (same as
`get_trigger`) and sets it paused so the scheduler stops firing it. Works
for both `trigger` and `cron` schedules. In-flight executions are
unaffected; this only gates new ones.

post.summary:
POST /tenants/:tenant_id/triggers/:name/resume — resume a paused schedule
(CLOACI-T-0749). Re-arms on the normal schedule; missed fires are not
caught up (skip policy).

get.summary: GET /tenants/:tenant_id/workflows — list registered workflows.

post.summary: POST /tenants/:tenant_id/workflows — multipart upload of .cloacina source package.

requestBody.description: The first file field is taken as the .cloacina package

403.description: Tenant/role denied or signature verification failed

get.summary: GET /tenants/:tenant_id/workflows/:name — get workflow details.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Package name, or package UUID to inspect pending/building/failed rows

post.description:
CLOACI-T-0580: execution is routed through `TenantRunnerCache`, which
returns (or constructs) a `DefaultRunner` bound to the tenant's
`Database`. The execution row + every event lands in the tenant's
schema, never the admin schema.

Blocks new executions of the workflow (manual and triggered) until resumed.
In-flight executions are unaffected. `name` may be the workflow name or
package name.

post.summary:
POST /tenants/:tenant_id/workflows/:name/resume — resume a paused workflow
(CLOACI-T-0749). New executions are accepted again.

get.summary: GET /tenants/:tenant_id/workflows/:name/source — read-only source view.
get.description:
Surfaces the original source retained in the package's `.cloacina` archive
(CLOACI-T-0750). `name` may be a package name or a package UUID, matching
`get_workflow`. Source is independent of build state, so it is available
even while a package is building or after a failed build.

200.description: Workflow unregistered. Idempotent: also returned when no matching package existed

AccountInfo.description: Public view of a local account (never the password hash).

error.description: Degradation detail when the source is unhealthy (e.g. connection error).

events_total.description: Total boundaries emitted since load (monotonic). `None` when untracked.

last_event_at.description:
Wall-clock of the last boundary this accumulator emitted (RFC3339), or
`None` if it hasn't emitted yet / the runtime predates freshness tracking.

last_operator_inject_at.description:
RFC 3339 time of the last operator inject, or `None` if never
(CLOACI-T-0776).

operator_injects.description:
Number of operator injects into this accumulator since load
(CLOACI-T-0776) — manual interventions via the REST/WS inject paths. `0`
when never injected. The UI marks injected accumulators with a "manual" pill.

reactor.description:
The reactor (graph) this accumulator feeds, self-registered by the graph
at load (CLOACI-I-0128 follow-up). `None` for older runtimes that didn't
register the descriptor. Lets an operator see what pushing to
`/v1/ws/accumulator/{name}` actually drives.

state.description:
Health state label (`live`/`socket_only`/`disconnected`/…), CLOACI-T-0765.
Mirrors the `state` inside `status`; promoted to a typed field for the UI.

status.description:
Accumulator health as reported by the endpoint registry. Free-form
JSON for now; structured in a later contract revision.

tenant_id.description: Owning tenant, or `None` for untagged single-tenant graphs.

AgentInfo.description: One registered execution agent in the in-memory fleet roster.

seconds_since_heartbeat.description:
Seconds since this agent's last heartbeat — the liveness signal an
operator reads (the underlying record stores a monotonic `Instant`,
not a wall-clock time).

tenant_id.description: Tenant scope the agent registered under, if any.

CompilerStatus.description:
Build-pipeline state, derived from the build queue in the database — the
same rows the compiler's own `/v1/status` reports. The server reads them
directly, so this needs no HTTP coupling to the compiler service.

last_failure_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent failed build, if any.

last_success_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent successful build, if any.

seconds_since_heartbeat.description:
Seconds since the compiler last claimed a build (its DB-visible
heartbeat). Only meaningful while a build is in flight.

status.description:
Coarse roll-up: `"building"` (work in flight), `"backlogged"` (packages
pending but none building — the compiler may be down), or `"idle"`
(nothing queued; liveness is undeterminable from the queue alone).

CreateAccountRequest.description: Create a local account in a tenant.

name.description:
Tenant name — doubles as schema name + database username.
Must be alphanumeric + underscore.

DeclaredSurface.description:
A declared injectable surface other than the workflow itself — a computation
graph, reactor, or accumulator (CLOACI-I-0128 Task D). Carries the surface's
declared input slots so the server can validate operator injections
(reactor fire / accumulator inject) and the UI can render typed forms.

Sourced from the package's `get_input_interface` FFI entrypoint at build
success and stored alongside the package metadata. An undeclared / untyped
surface has `slots` whose schemas are permissive (`{}`).

name.description: Surface name (graph name / reactor name / accumulator name).

ErrorBody.description:
Standardized error response body. Every non-2xx response from
`cloacina-server` carries this shape; the request correlation ID is in
the `x-request-id` response header, not the body.

context.description: Optional JSON context to pass to the workflow.

ExecuteResponse.description: `202 Accepted` body for a scheduled workflow execution.

ExecutionEvent.description: One row in the execution event log.

event_data.description: JSON-encoded additional data for the event; `null` when absent.

task_name.description:
Local name of the task this event is about, resolved from the event's
`task_execution_id`. `null` for workflow-scoped events (e.g.
`workflow_completed`) or when the task can't be resolved
(CLOACI-I-0124 / WS-9).

ExecutionSummary.description: One row in the executions list.

trigger_origin.description:
How the run was triggered (CLOACI-T-0776): `"manual"` for an operator run
via the REST execute endpoint; `null` for cron/trigger/reactor-driven runs.
The UI marks manual runs with a "manual" pill.

FireMode.description:
How a manual REST fire should populate the reactor's input cache.

CLOACI-T-0751. Mirrors the two WS write commands (`ForceFire` /
`FireWith`) but with operator-friendly, typed input.

FireReactorRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/health/reactors/{name}/fire` (CLOACI-T-0751).

Operators supply typed JSON per source; the server serializes each value
to the boundary wire encoding so callers never deal in raw `Vec<u8>`.

inputs.description:
Per-source typed payloads, keyed by accumulator source name. Each
JSON value is serialized to the boundary encoding server-side.
Required (and non-empty) when `mode` is `fire_with`; ignored for
`force_fire`. Each value may be any JSON.

mode.description:
Whether to fire with the current cache (`force_fire`) or to inject
`inputs` first (`fire_with`). Defaults to `force_fire`.

FireReactorResponse.description: Response body for a successful manual reactor fire (CLOACI-T-0751).

reactor.description: Echoes the reactor name that was fired.

sources_injected.description: Source names whose values were injected (empty for `force_fire`).

FireTriggerRequest.description:
`POST /tenants/{tenant_id}/triggers/{name}/fire` request (CLOACI-T-0777).
Manually push an event to a trigger; it fans out to every subscribed workflow.

event.description:
Optional typed event merged into each fired workflow's context, validated
against the trigger's declared params (CLOACI-T-0777 P2). Omit to fire with
just the trigger metadata.

fired.description: How many subscribed workflows were fired (the fan-out count).

FiredExecution.description: One workflow fired by a manual trigger fire (CLOACI-T-0777).

GraphStatus.description:
One row in `GET /v1/health/graphs`, and the `GET /v1/health/graphs/{name}`
response body.

accumulators.description: Names of the accumulators feeding this graph.

fires.description:
Total graph fires since load — the reactor's live fire counter
(CLOACI-I-0124 / WS-10). The UI derives recent throughput from the delta
across successive polls.

health.description:
Graph health snapshot; `{"state": "running" | "stopped"}` when no
detailed health is available. Free-form JSON for now.

input_strategy.description: Input strategy of the bound reactor: `"latest"` | `"sequential"`.

last_fired_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the last graph fire; `null` if it hasn't fired yet.

paused.description: Pause state of the graph's reactor.

reaction_mode.description: Reaction mode of the bound reactor: `"when_any"` | `"when_all"`.

reactor.description: Name of the reactor this graph is bound to (the trigger that fires it).

source_package.description:
Package whose retained source defines this graph's nodes/reactor, so the
UI can fetch it via `GET /workflows/{package}/source` and show node code
(CLOACI-T-0773). `None` when the package can't be resolved (e.g. a graph
declaring no typed surface). Populated on the single-graph detail endpoint
only; the list leaves it `None`.

oneOf.description:
Node/edge topology of the computation graph, for rendering its DAG.
`None` for graphs predating topology emission. (CLOACI-T-0673)

GraphTopology.description: Node/edge topology of a computation graph (CLOACI-T-0673).

GraphTopologyEdge.description: One directed edge in a computation graph (CLOACI-T-0673).

label.description: Routing-variant label for conditional edges; `None` for linear edges.

GraphTopologyNode.description: One compute node in a computation graph (CLOACI-T-0673).

inputs.description: Accumulator names this node reads from the input cache (entry nodes).

InjectAccumulatorRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/health/accumulators/{name}/inject` (CLOACI-T-0753)
— push a single typed event into a running accumulator, the operator-facing
REST analogue of the WS accumulator-push path. The JSON `event` is serialized
to the boundary wire encoding server-side, so operators never craft raw
`Vec<u8>`.

event.description: The event payload (any JSON) to push to the accumulator.

InjectAccumulatorResponse.description: Response body for a successful accumulator inject (CLOACI-T-0753).

accumulator.description: Echoes the accumulator name the event was pushed to.

delivered.description: Number of receivers the event was delivered to.

InputSlot.description:
One declared input slot of an injectable surface: a named, typed value the
surface accepts. `schema` is a JSON Schema fragment (the type descriptor —
`schemars`-derived for Rust, type-hint-derived for Python) that the UI can
render a form from and the server can validate an injection against.

`required` slots must be supplied; `default` (when present) is applied when a
slot is omitted. A surface with no declared interface exposes an empty slot
list (the "undeclared" state) and accepts free-form input.

default.description: Optional default applied when the slot is omitted.

name.description:
Slot name — the context key (workflows) or source/event name
(accumulators/reactors).

required.description: Whether this slot must be supplied for the injection to be accepted.

schema.description: JSON Schema fragment describing the accepted value's type.

KeyCreatedResponse.description:
`201 Created` body for a new API key. The plaintext `key` is returned
exactly once — it cannot be retrieved again.

is_admin.description: God-mode flag — never granted via the create endpoints.

KeyInfo.description: One row in the key list (`GET /auth/keys`). No hashes or plaintext.

ListResponse_AccountInfo.description:
Unified list envelope (CLOACI-T-0594 / API-03): every list endpoint
returns `{items, total}`. `total` is best-effort — it equals the
returned page size when the server doesn't run a separate COUNT.

items.description: Public view of a local account (never the password hash).

ListResponse_AccumulatorStatus.description:
Unified list envelope (CLOACI-T-0594 / API-03): every list endpoint
returns `{items, total}`. `total` is best-effort — it equals the
returned page size when the server doesn't run a separate COUNT.

error.description: Degradation detail when the source is unhealthy (e.g. connection error).

events_total.description: Total boundaries emitted since load (monotonic). `None` when untracked.

last_event_at.description:
Wall-clock of the last boundary this accumulator emitted (RFC3339), or
`None` if it hasn't emitted yet / the runtime predates freshness tracking.

last_operator_inject_at.description:
RFC 3339 time of the last operator inject, or `None` if never
(CLOACI-T-0776).

operator_injects.description:
Number of operator injects into this accumulator since load
(CLOACI-T-0776) — manual interventions via the REST/WS inject paths. `0`
when never injected. The UI marks injected accumulators with a "manual" pill.

reactor.description:
The reactor (graph) this accumulator feeds, self-registered by the graph
at load (CLOACI-I-0128 follow-up). `None` for older runtimes that didn't
register the descriptor. Lets an operator see what pushing to
`/v1/ws/accumulator/{name}` actually drives.

state.description:
Health state label (`live`/`socket_only`/`disconnected`/…), CLOACI-T-0765.
Mirrors the `state` inside `status`; promoted to a typed field for the UI.

status.description:
Accumulator health as reported by the endpoint registry. Free-form
JSON for now; structured in a later contract revision.

tenant_id.description: Owning tenant, or `None` for untagged single-tenant graphs.

ListResponse_AgentInfo.description:
Unified list envelope (CLOACI-T-0594 / API-03): every list endpoint
returns `{items, total}`. `total` is best-effort — it equals the
returned page size when the server doesn't run a separate COUNT.

items.description: One registered execution agent in the in-memory fleet roster.

seconds_since_heartbeat.description:
Seconds since this agent's last heartbeat — the liveness signal an
operator reads (the underlying record stores a monotonic `Instant`,
not a wall-clock time).

tenant_id.description: Tenant scope the agent registered under, if any.

ListResponse_GraphStatus.description:
Unified list envelope (CLOACI-T-0594 / API-03): every list endpoint
returns `{items, total}`. `total` is best-effort — it equals the
returned page size when the server doesn't run a separate COUNT.

items.description:
One row in `GET /v1/health/graphs`, and the `GET /v1/health/graphs/{name}`
response body.

accumulators.description: Names of the accumulators feeding this graph.

fires.description:
Total graph fires since load — the reactor's live fire counter
(CLOACI-I-0124 / WS-10). The UI derives recent throughput from the delta
across successive polls.

health.description:
Graph health snapshot; `{"state": "running" | "stopped"}` when no
detailed health is available. Free-form JSON for now.

input_strategy.description: Input strategy of the bound reactor: `"latest"` | `"sequential"`.

last_fired_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the last graph fire; `null` if it hasn't fired yet.

paused.description: Pause state of the graph's reactor.

reaction_mode.description: Reaction mode of the bound reactor: `"when_any"` | `"when_all"`.

reactor.description: Name of the reactor this graph is bound to (the trigger that fires it).

source_package.description:
Package whose retained source defines this graph's nodes/reactor, so the
UI can fetch it via `GET /workflows/{package}/source` and show node code
(CLOACI-T-0773). `None` when the package can't be resolved (e.g. a graph
declaring no typed surface). Populated on the single-graph detail endpoint
only; the list leaves it `None`.

oneOf.description:
Node/edge topology of the computation graph, for rendering its DAG.
`None` for graphs predating topology emission. (CLOACI-T-0673)

ListResponse_KeyInfo.description:
Unified list envelope (CLOACI-T-0594 / API-03): every list endpoint
returns `{items, total}`. `total` is best-effort — it equals the
returned page size when the server doesn't run a separate COUNT.

items.description: One row in the key list (`GET /auth/keys`). No hashes or plaintext.

ListResponse_ReactorFire.description:
Unified list envelope (CLOACI-T-0594 / API-03): every list endpoint
returns `{items, total}`. `total` is best-effort — it equals the
returned page size when the server doesn't run a separate COUNT.

items.description:
One recorded reactor fire (CLOACI-T-0766) — a row in
`GET /v1/health/reactors/{name}/fires`. Makes fires observable (outcome +
duration), not just counted.

duration_ms.description: Graph execution wall-clock for this fire, in milliseconds.

error.description: Error detail for a failed fire.

inputs.description:
Input boundary values that triggered this fire: source name → value
(CLOACI-T-0775). The graph's I/O history, so a fire reads as more than
"ran in 0ms".

manual.description:
Whether this fire was a manual operator intervention
(`force_fire`/`fire_with`) rather than a criteria-driven fire over real
boundary events (CLOACI-T-0776). The UI marks it with a "manual" pill.

ok.description: Whether the graph execution completed (`false` = errored).

outputs.description:
Terminal outputs the graph produced for this fire, as JSON
(CLOACI-T-0775). Empty when the executor can't serialize them (e.g. the
Python reactor path) or on a failed fire.

ListResponse_ReactorStatus.description:
Unified list envelope (CLOACI-T-0594 / API-03): every list endpoint
returns `{items, total}`. `total` is best-effort — it equals the
returned page size when the server doesn't run a separate COUNT.

items.description:
One row in `GET /v1/health/reactors` (CLOACI-T-0742). Reactor-first view:
reactors are standalone (a graph binds to a reactor, not vice versa), so a
reactor with no graph bound appears here but not in `GET /v1/health/graphs`.

bound_graphs.description: Graphs bound to this reactor; empty when the reactor has no graph yet.

fires.description: Total fires since load (the reactor's live fire counter, WS-10).

health.description:
Reactor health snapshot; `{"state": "running" | "stopped"}` when no
detailed health is available. Free-form JSON, mirroring `GraphStatus`.

last_fired_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the last fire; `null` if it hasn't fired yet.

ListResponse_TenantSummary.description:
Unified list envelope (CLOACI-T-0594 / API-03): every list endpoint
returns `{items, total}`. `total` is best-effort — it equals the
returned page size when the server doesn't run a separate COUNT.

items.description: One row in the tenant list (`GET /tenants`).

LocalLoginRequest.description:
A local-login attempt. `tenant` selects which tenant's account namespace to
authenticate against (`None` = a global account).

LocalLoginResponse.description:
A successful local login. `key` is the minted bearer key — shown exactly
once; the SPA stores it (sessionStorage) and presents it as `Bearer`.

PackageUploadForm.description:
Multipart form for workflow package upload. Spec-only type: the handler
accepts the first file field regardless of name; `file` is the
conventional field name.

ReactorFire.description:
One recorded reactor fire (CLOACI-T-0766) — a row in
`GET /v1/health/reactors/{name}/fires`. Makes fires observable (outcome +
duration), not just counted.

duration_ms.description: Graph execution wall-clock for this fire, in milliseconds.

error.description: Error detail for a failed fire.

inputs.description:
Input boundary values that triggered this fire: source name → value
(CLOACI-T-0775). The graph's I/O history, so a fire reads as more than
"ran in 0ms".

manual.description:
Whether this fire was a manual operator intervention
(`force_fire`/`fire_with`) rather than a criteria-driven fire over real
boundary events (CLOACI-T-0776). The UI marks it with a "manual" pill.

ok.description: Whether the graph execution completed (`false` = errored).

outputs.description:
Terminal outputs the graph produced for this fire, as JSON
(CLOACI-T-0775). Empty when the executor can't serialize them (e.g. the
Python reactor path) or on a failed fire.

ReactorFireTimeseries.description:
`GET /v1/health/reactors/{name}/fires/timeseries` (CLOACI-T-0766): fire counts
per minute for the last 60 minutes, oldest → newest, gaps filled with 0.

buckets.description: 60 per-minute fire counts, oldest first; the last entry is the current minute.

ReactorStatus.description:
One row in `GET /v1/health/reactors` (CLOACI-T-0742). Reactor-first view:
reactors are standalone (a graph binds to a reactor, not vice versa), so a
reactor with no graph bound appears here but not in `GET /v1/health/graphs`.

bound_graphs.description: Graphs bound to this reactor; empty when the reactor has no graph yet.

fires.description: Total fires since load (the reactor's live fire counter, WS-10).

health.description:
Reactor health snapshot; `{"state": "running" | "stopped"}` when no
detailed health is available. Free-form JSON, mirroring `GraphStatus`.

last_fired_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the last fire; `null` if it hasn't fired yet.

ResetPasswordRequest.description: Reset a local account's password (admin-reset-only, OQ-12).

TaskExecutionDetail.description: One per-task row of an execution (CLOACI-I-0124 / WS-1).

created_at.description:
Row-created timestamp (RFC 3339) — always present; a fallback "start"
when `started_at` is null (some runner configs don't stamp it).

last_error.description: Last error message for the most recent failed attempt, when present.

started_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp; `null` until the task starts.

updated_at.description: Row-updated timestamp (RFC 3339) — always present; a fallback "end".

TenantCreatedResponse.description:
`201 Created` body for a new tenant. Password and connection string are
intentionally excluded to prevent credential leakage (SEC-08).

TenantListResponse_ExecutionSummary.description:
List envelope variant that retains a top-level `tenant_id`, used by
tenant-scoped list endpoints for backward compatibility with operator
dashboards that key off it.

items.description: One row in the executions list.

trigger_origin.description:
How the run was triggered (CLOACI-T-0776): `"manual"` for an operator run
via the REST execute endpoint; `null` for cron/trigger/reactor-driven runs.
The UI marks manual runs with a "manual" pill.

TenantListResponse_TriggerScheduleSummar.description:
List envelope variant that retains a top-level `tenant_id`, used by
tenant-scoped list endpoints for backward compatibility with operator
dashboards that key off it.

items.description: One row in the trigger list.

paused.description:
Whether the schedule is paused (CLOACI-T-0749). A paused schedule is not
fired by the scheduler until resumed; distinct from `enabled`.

paused_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of when it was paused, if paused.

TenantListResponse_WorkflowSummary.description:
List envelope variant that retains a top-level `tenant_id`, used by
tenant-scoped list endpoints for backward compatibility with operator
dashboards that key off it.

items.description: One row in the workflow list (`GET /tenants/{tenant_id}/workflows`).

paused.description:
Whether this workflow is paused (CLOACI-T-0749). Paused workflows refuse
new executions until resumed.

tasks.description: Task IDs included in this package.

workflow_name.description:
Executable workflow name (the identifier to execute by). Differs from
`package_name` under the standard convention (package `demo-slow-rust`
→ workflow `demo_slow_workflow`). Falls back to `package_name` for
packages predating workflow-name persistence. (CLOACI-T-0671)

revoked_keys.description: Number of still-active API keys revoked during teardown.

TenantSummary.description: One row in the tenant list (`GET /tenants`).

TriggerExecution.description: One row in `recent_executions` of the trigger detail response.

name.description: The name the schedule was addressed by (trigger or workflow name).

paused.description: Current paused state after the operation.

TriggerScheduleInfo.description: Schedule fields in the trigger detail response.

paused_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of when it was paused, if paused.

poll_interval_ms.description:
Poll interval in milliseconds for `trigger`-type schedules (the
custom-poll cadence). `None` for cron schedules. Lets the Triggers
detail view show "polls every Ns" (CLOACI-I-0124 / WS-6).

TriggerScheduleSummary.description: One row in the trigger list.

paused.description:
Whether the schedule is paused (CLOACI-T-0749). A paused schedule is not
fired by the scheduler until resumed; distinct from `enabled`.

paused_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of when it was paused, if paused.

WorkflowDetail.description:
`GET /tenants/{tenant_id}/workflows/{name}` response — summary fields
plus real build state (pending/building/failed/success).

declared_params.description:
CLOACI-I-0128: declared input params (named, JSON-Schema-typed slots) the
workflow accepts at execute time. Empty when undeclared. Lets the UI
render a typed execute form and the server validate context.

paused.description:
Whether this workflow is paused (CLOACI-T-0749). Paused workflows refuse
new executions until resumed.

task_graph.description:
The task dependency graph (nodes + their upstream dependencies) for
rendering the full workflow DAG. Empty for packages predating
task-graph persistence. (CLOACI-T-0663)

tasks.description: Task IDs included in this package.

workflow_name.description:
Executable workflow name (the identifier to execute by). Differs from
`package_name` under the standard convention (package `demo-slow-rust`
→ workflow `demo_slow_workflow`). Falls back to `package_name` for
packages predating workflow-name persistence. (CLOACI-T-0671)

id.description: Package UUID of the affected workflow.

name.description: The name the workflow was addressed by (workflow or package name).

paused.description: Current paused state after the operation.

WorkflowSourceFile.description:
One source file from a workflow package's retained `.cloacina` archive,
surfaced read-only for display (CLOACI-T-0750).

language.description:
Best-effort language id derived from the file extension (`"rust"`,
`"python"`, `"toml"`, …), for syntax highlighting. `None` when unknown.

path.description:
Path relative to the package source root, using forward slashes
(e.g. `"src/lib.rs"`, `"package.toml"`).

WorkflowSourceResponse.description:
`GET /tenants/{tenant_id}/workflows/{name}/source` response — the original
source retained in the package's `.cloacina` archive, surfaced read-only
(CLOACI-T-0750). The source is independent of build state, so it is
available even for packages that are still building or failed to build.

files.description:
Source files in the package, sorted by path. Binary and oversized files
are omitted.

WorkflowSummary.description: One row in the workflow list (`GET /tenants/{tenant_id}/workflows`).

paused.description:
Whether this workflow is paused (CLOACI-T-0749). Paused workflows refuse
new executions until resumed.

tasks.description: Task IDs included in this package.

workflow_name.description:
Executable workflow name (the identifier to execute by). Differs from
`package_name` under the standard convention (package `demo-slow-rust`
→ workflow `demo_slow_workflow`). Falls back to `package_name` for
packages predating workflow-name persistence. (CLOACI-T-0671)

WorkflowTaskNode.description:
One node in a workflow's task dependency graph — a task plus the ids of the
tasks it depends on. The UI renders these as a DAG. (CLOACI-T-0663)

dependencies.description: Local ids of the tasks this task depends on (its incoming edges).

doc_what.description:
CLOACI-T-0752 "what" — short summary parsed from the task's
doc-comment/docstring at build time. `None` when undocumented.

doc_why.description:
CLOACI-T-0752 "why" — rationale parsed from the doc-comment/docstring.
`None` when undocumented.

id.description: Local task id (the node id), e.g. `"validate"`.

WorkflowUploadedResponse.description:
`201 Created` body for a workflow package upload
(`POST /tenants/{tenant_id}/workflows`, multipart).

WsTicketResponse.description:
`POST /auth/ws-ticket` response — a single-use, short-lived ticket for
WebSocket upgrade auth (avoids long-lived API keys in URLs).

api_key.description: Cloacina API key, sent as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. Tenant scope is carried by the key itself; tenant-scoped routes additionally name the tenant in the path.
